basic_details.job_title: UKSSSC Recruitment 2026: 398 Kanisth Sahayak & Stenographer Grade-1/PA Vacancies - Apply Online
basic_details.department: Subordinate Courts (Civil & Family Courts) under Nainital High Court, Uttarakhand
basic_details.short_information: The Uttarakhand Subordinate Services Selection Commission (UKSSSC) has released a fresh recruitment notification on 15 June 2026 to fill 398 vacancies of Kanisth Sahayak (Junior Assistant) and Stenographer Grade-1/Personal Assistant in the Civil Courts and Family Courts functioning under the Nainital High Court, Uttarakhand. Eligible candidates can apply online at www.sssc.uk.gov.in between 22 June 2026 and 6 July 2026. This article explains every important detail of the notification, including vacancy distribution, eligibility conditions, exam pattern, fee structure, and the complete Apply On

application_fee.details: The examination fee varies by category and must be paid online only, through credit card, debit card, net banking, or UPI.

age_limit.details: Age eligibility is calculated as on a fixed reference date, and several categories receive age relaxation, which is summarised below. | The age of candidates is calculated as on 1 January 2026. Candidates must be between 21 and 35 years, i.e., born between 2 January 1991 and 1 January 2005 (both dates inclusive).

qualification.details: A few additional eligibility conditions from the notification are summarised here for quick reference. | Nationality: Only Indian citizens are eligible for appointment to clerical cadre posts. Marital Status: A male candidate with more than one living spouse, or a female candidate who has married a man with a living first wife, is not eligible. Physical Fitness: Candidates must be mentally and physically fit and will need to clear a medical board examination before final confirmation of appointment. Character Certificate: Required from the head of the institution last attended and two respectable persons unrelated to the candidate. No Objection Certificate: Mandatory for candidates currently in government/semi-government service. Anti-Cheating Provisions: The Uttarakhand Competitive Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2023 applies to this examination; any unfair means used will invite action under this Act, including debarment from future UKSSSC examinations and FIR. Prohibited Items: Cameras, calculators, mobile phones, pagers, scanners, and any electronic/communication device are strictly prohibited inside the examination centre.

selection_process: Selection is based purely on a written objective-type competitive examination
followed by document/record verification. This section explains the marking scheme and qualifying criteria.
The written exam will be of 140 marks

covering General Knowledge and General Studies. A separate typing/shorthand skill test worth 60 marks will also be conducted; the detailed syllabus will be published separately on the UKSSSC website. Minimum qualifying marks: 45% for General and OBC category candidates
and 35% for SC/ST category candidates. Candidates scoring below this will be considered unqualified. Each question carries 4 answer options; 1 mark is awarded for each correct answer

and 0.25 marks (1/4) are deducted for each incorrect answer. Marking more than one option for a question is treated as a wrong answer and attracts negative marking. No negative marking applies to unattempted questions. The typing/shorthand test is qualifying in nature. If two candidates score equal marks in the written exam and equal marks in Hindi typing/shorthand
preference is given to the one who also qualifies in English typing/shorthand. If a tie still remains
the older candidate is ranked higher in the merit list.
how_to_apply: Interested and eligible candidates can complete the Apply Online process on the official UKSSSC website in five simple steps between 22 June 2026 and 6 July 2026.
Registration & Personal Details – Register using a unique mobile number and email ID (each can be used only once) and fill in personal details.
Educational & Other Details – Enter educational qualification, category, district preference, and other required information.
Upload Photo, Signature & Thumb Impression – Upload the scanned photograph, signature, and left thumb impression as per the specified size.
Make Payment – Pay the applicable examination fee online via credit/debit card, net banking, or UPI.
Print Application – Download and print a copy of the final submitted application for personal record; it does not need to be submitted to the Commission at this stage.

Candidates get a one-time opportunity to edit their online application between 8 July 2026 and 10 July 2026 after the closing date, but corrections to name, parents' name, date of birth, and examination centre will not be permitted. Applicants are advised to Apply Online well before the last date, since heavy website traffic near the deadline may prevent timely submission.

faq.question: Q1. What is UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: UKSSSC Recruitment 2026 is a notification released by the Uttarakhand Subordinate Services Selection Commission on 15 June 2026 to fill 398 posts of Kanisth Sahayak and Stenographer Grade-1/Personal Assistant in the Civil and Family Courts under the Nainital High Court, Uttarakhand.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 398 total vacancies: 259 posts of Kanisth Sahayak (230 in Civil Courts and 29 in Family Courts) and 139 posts of Stenographer Grade-1/Personal Assistant (128 in Civil Courts and 11 in Family Courts).

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply online for UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process starts on 22 June 2026, and the last date to submit the application is 6 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for UKSSSC Kanisth Sahayak and Stenographer posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 21 and 35 years of age as on 1 January 2026, with age relaxation available for SC/ST/OBC, persons with disabilities, freedom fighter dependents, and ex-servicemen of Uttarakhand as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification for UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a graduate degree from a recognised university, with good knowledge of Hindi and English. Kanisth Sahayak posts require a Hindi typing speed of 25 words per minute, while Stenographer posts require 80 words per minute in Hindi shorthand and 25 words per minute in Hindi typing.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs 300 for Unreserved/OBC candidates, Rs 150 for SC/ST/EWS and disabled candidates of Uttarakhand, and Rs 0 (no fee) for orphan candidates.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for Kanisth Sahayak and Stenographer posts?
faq.answer: Kanisth Sahayak posts carry a pay scale of Rs 21,700–69,100 (Level-3). Stenographer Grade-1/Personal Assistant posts carry Rs 29,200–92,300 (Level-5) in Civil Courts and Rs 44,900–1,42,400 (Level-7) in Family Courts.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on a 140-mark written objective-type competitive examination covering General Knowledge and General Studies, followed by a 60-mark qualifying typing/shorthand test and document verification.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply online for UKSSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can Apply Online at www.sssc.uk.gov.in by completing five steps: registration, filling educational details, uploading photo/signature/thumb impression, making the fee payment, and printing the final application.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the UKSSSC Recruitment 2026 notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification is available as a PDF Download Link on the UKSSSC website, www.sssc.uk.gov.in, along with the application portal and detailed annexures for category certificates.
